seoTitle: How to Add a Domain in Cloudflare (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Onboard a new domain into Cloudflare: import existing DNS records automatically, pick the free plan, and switch nameservers at your registrar.
intro: Getting a domain onto Cloudflare starts with letting the platform scan and import your existing DNS setup so nothing breaks in the switch. This guide walks through onboarding a domain, choosing the free plan, and pointing your registrar's nameservers to Cloudflare.

steps.heading: Start onboarding a domain
steps.body: From the Cloudflare dashboard, click 'Onboard a Domain' and enter your clean root domain name.

steps.heading: Select the Free plan
steps.body: Scroll through the plan options and choose the free tier to get started without cost.

steps.heading: Review the automated DNS scan
steps.body: Let Cloudflare's Quick Scan engine discover and import your existing records, then check that mail (MX) rows and hosting A or CNAME entries were captured correctly.

steps.heading: Copy your assigned nameservers
steps.body: Locate the unique pair of Cloudflare nameservers assigned to your account and copy them to your clipboard.

steps.heading: Update nameservers at your registrar
steps.body: Log into your external registrar's control panel, replace the old nameservers with Cloudflare's, and run a verification check back in Cloudflare to confirm activation.

faq.question: Will adding my domain to Cloudflare break my existing email?
faq.answer: As long as the automated DNS scan correctly imports your MX records before you switch nameservers, email routing should continue working normally.

faq.question: Is the free Cloudflare plan enough for a basic website?
faq.answer: The free plan includes core DNS management, proxy caching, and basic security features, which is sufficient for many small sites.

faq.question: How long does nameserver propagation take after switching to Cloudflare?
faq.answer: Nameserver changes can take anywhere from a few minutes to 24 hours or more to fully propagate globally, depending on your registrar and previous DNS TTL settings.

faq.question: What if the DNS scan misses some of my records?
faq.answer: You can manually add any missing records directly in the DNS Records panel before or after switching your nameservers.
